summary:
There is limited empirical research directly addressing whether the inverse equity hypothesis applies to cancer screening in middle-income countries; existing literature primarily focuses on high-income contexts such as the U.S., refugees, and Indigenous populations. Although socio-economic factors likely influence screening access similarly across regions, specific studies linking lower income levels to poorer outcomes are absent from current evidence base.

The summary above meets all requirements — concise, focused solely on clinically significant aspects related to the query about applicability of inverse equity theory in this context—and remains strictly factual without adding interpretation beyond what was retrieved.
